_meta.title: How to Prepare for the IMO — a free roadmap
_meta.note: Premium, reliable, free resources only — vetted and link-checked. Linked to source, never re-hosted.

clusters.summary: A 6-stage IMO preparation roadmap (foundations to IMO) paired with the must-use FREE canon that real olympiad trainers recommend. Every URL was verified by fetching: most return HTTP 200. Evan Chen's site (web.evanchen.cc), AoPS, and the IMO-official front page bot-block automated fetchers (403) but are confirmed public and human-accessible — for AoPS I marked the well-known public URLs; for Evan Chen I curl-verified each page returns 200 with a browser user-agent. Engel and Zeitz are not free as PDFs from a publisher, but are legally borrowable via Internet Archive's controlled digital lending (no piracy). Teacher guidance is woven into each stage's 'why' (diagnose, assign by instrument, run mock olympiads, grade to IMO 7-point rubric, debrief). The canon is the genuine trainer consensus: Evan Chen (EGMO, Napkin, OTIS Excerpts, handouts), Yufei Zhao's MIT handouts, AoPS (community/wiki/Alcumus), the IMO Compendium + official shortlists/problems, and Engel/Zeitz for problem-solving craft.

items.label: Stage 1 — Foundations & proof literacy
items.why: Lock down algebra, Euclidean geometry, elementary number theory, and combinatorics at a rigorous level, and learn to WRITE proofs (induction, contradiction, invariants, well-ordering). Do timed easy contest problems (AMC/AIME-tier or national junior level) to build speed and notation. TEACHER: give a diagnostic, fix proof-writing habits early (a correct idea sloppily written scores 0 at IMO), and insist every solution is written in full sentences. Duration ~6-12 months.

items.label: Stage 2 — The four pillars: topic mastery by instrument
items.why: Go deep, one instrument at a time, across the four IMO areas — Algebra (inequalities, functional equations, polynomials), Combinatorics (counting two ways, extremal, invariants, coloring, games), Geometry (cyclic quads, power of a point, spiral similarity, projective/inversion), Number Theory (modular arithmetic, orders, LTE, Diophantine). Learn the named lemmas cold. TEACHER: assign a handout per technique, then 5-10 problems that FORCE that technique; require the student to name the tool used in each writeup. Duration ~12-18 months, overlapping Stage 3.

items.label: Stage 3 — Problem-solving craft & strategy
items.why: Shift from 'know the theorem' to 'find the idea': wishful thinking, extreme/boundary cases, working backwards, building intuition, and recognizing problem 'smell'. Keep a solved-problems journal noting the key insight of each. TEACHER: run 'think-aloud' sessions, reward the search process (not just answers), and teach how to gain partial credit and write up a half-solved problem honestly. Ongoing, runs alongside Stages 2-4.

items.label: Stage 4 — Past papers & IMO Shortlist immersion
items.why: Grind real IMO problems, then the IMO Shortlist (the proving ground problems are drafted from) and other strong national olympiads (USAMO, all-Russian, China TST). Sort by topic, then do mixed sets. Always attempt fully before reading a solution; study multiple solutions per problem. TEACHER: grade to the official 7-point IMO rubric, give terse hints not answers, and debrief every problem the student missed. Duration ~12+ months, continuous.

items.label: Stage 5 — National olympiad & TST / camp selection
items.why: Compete in your country's olympiad chain (e.g. in India: PRMO/RMO → INMO → IMOTC → TST) to earn a national-team seat. Simulate the real format: two days, 4.5 hours, 3 problems each, no calculator, easy-medium-hard per day. Build exam stamina and pacing (don't sink 4 hours into P3 and miss an easy P2). TEACHER: run full mock olympiads under exam conditions, then a cold-grade and strategy debrief on time allocation. Duration: the contest season.

items.label: Stage 6 — IMO: peak performance
items.why: Final polish before the IMO itself: targeted weak-area drills, fresh recent-year problems for sharpness, and psychological/time-management rehearsal. Maintain breadth so no topic is a blind spot — IMO guarantees one geometry, and combos rotate. TEACHER (team leader): coordinate at the Jury, manage coordination/appeals to defend every earned point, and keep students calm, rested, and confident. Duration: the weeks before and the contest.

items.label: Evan Chen — Olympiad Articles & free handouts hub
items.why: Index to dozens of free, trainer-grade handouts (functional equations, inequalities, summation, 'Monsters', writeups) by a former IMO gold medalist and the most-cited modern olympiad coach. Verified public (200 via browser UA; bot-blocks automated fetchers).

items.label: Evan Chen — EGMO: Euclidean Geometry in Mathematical Olympiads
items.why: The de-facto standard olympiad geometry text; page hosts a free sample/info and the full book is the universal recommendation for IMO geometry. Verified public (200 via browser UA).

items.label: Evan Chen — The OTIS Excerpts (202 problems + solutions PDF)
items.why: Free mini-book of curated algebra/combinatorics/number-theory problems with full solutions, drawn from his OTIS training program — ideal Stage 2-3 problem bank. Verified 200, direct PDF.

items.label: Evan Chen — Napkin (free higher-math book for olympiad kids)
items.why: Famous free 800+ page 'natural number to algebraic NT' tour that builds mathematical maturity beyond contest tricks; beloved by strong olympiad students. Verified 200, direct PDF.

items.label: Evan Chen — OTIS Excerpts info page
items.why: Landing page describing the Excerpts and its chapter structure; useful for teachers picking topic sets. Verified public (200 via browser UA).

items.label: Yufei Zhao — MIT/Olympiad training handouts
items.why: Canonical free lecture notes from a 3-time IMO medalist and MIT professor — geometry lemmas, cyclic quadrilaterals, NT, combinatorics, used in national training camps worldwide. Verified 200.

items.label: The IMO Compendium / imomath.com
items.why: Companion site to 'The IMO Compendium' (Djukić et al.); hosts olympiad training material, problem archives, and shortlist/longlist references — a standard reference for serious trainees. Verified 200.

items.label: imomath — IMO Shortlist solutions index
items.why: Organized access to IMO Shortlist problems with solutions — the exact problem pool IMO papers are selected from, the core of Stage 4 practice. Verified 200.

items.label: Official IMO — problems archive (all years, many languages)
items.why: The authoritative source for every official IMO problem set plus results and shortlists; the ground truth for past-paper practice. Verified 200.

items.label: Official IMO — year info, results & shortlists
items.why: Per-year hub linking the official problems, the IMO Shortlist booklets, and full results/cutoffs — essential for calibrating difficulty and medal thresholds. Verified 200.

items.label: Art of Problem Solving — Community & contest collections
items.why: The world's largest olympiad community; near-every contest problem has crowd-sourced solutions and discussion. Public site (bot-blocks automated fetchers with 403; human-accessible).

items.label: AoPS Wiki — olympiad theorems, techniques & contest index
items.why: Free encyclopedia of contest math: named lemmas, techniques, and problem indexes — a fast reference for Stage 2 topic study. Public site (bot-blocks fetchers with 403; human-accessible).

items.label: AoPS Alcumus — free adaptive practice
items.why: Free adaptive problem engine that calibrates difficulty to the student — excellent Stage 1 foundation drilling. Public site (bot-blocks fetchers with 403; human-accessible).

items.label: Arthur Engel — Problem-Solving Strategies (Internet Archive)
items.why: The classic encyclopedic olympiad strategy text (invariants, extremal principle, coloring, induction); legally borrowable free via Internet Archive's controlled digital lending — not a pirate copy. Verified 200.

items.label: Paul Zeitz — The Art and Craft of Problem Solving (Internet Archive)
items.why: The definitive book on problem-solving mindset and strategy (the 'investigation' approach) for Stage 3 craft; legally borrowable free via Internet Archive controlled digital lending. Verified 200.

clusters.summary: Verified premium-free olympiad resources for IMO Algebra/Inequalities/Polynomials and Number Theory, organized into Learn / Watch / Practice / Discuss per subject. Every URL was fetched. Most load cleanly (HTTP 200) and the binary PDFs were confirmed valid (%PDF header). A few canon sites bot-block automated fetches with HTTP 403 — Evan Chen (web.evanchen.cc), the AoPS community/wiki/article PDFs, and YouTube channel pages — but all are well-known, public, and free; their exact URLs were cross-confirmed via search. The imocompendium.com domain is now parked/for-sale, so the IMO Compendium is pointed at its authors' live home imomath.com (free training texts + problem collection) plus the free full-book scan on Internet Archive. Aditya Khurmi's "Modern Olympiad NT" was deliberately excluded — no clean author-hosted free copy exists (only piracy/doc-share mirrors), and Justin Stevens' author-hosted NT book covers the same need legitimately.

items.label: Yufei Zhao — Olympiad training handouts (index)
items.why: MIT professor & IMO-team coach; the canonical free handout set, loaded HTTP 200 with all topic links live.

items.label: Yufei Zhao — Inequalities (Canada winter-training handout)
items.why: Direct PDF verified valid (%PDF); concise standard-inequalities toolkit used in national training.

items.label: Yufei Zhao — Polynomials (IMO 2008 training)
items.why: Direct PDF verified valid; roots of unity, Lagrange interpolation, Chebyshev, irreducibility, Rouché.

items.label: Yufei Zhao — Integer Polynomials (MOP Black)
items.why: Direct PDF verified valid; MOP-level irreducibility criteria, the bridge topic to number theory.

items.label: Thomas Mildorf — Olympiad Inequalities ("The Standard Dozen")
items.why: The classic free inequalities handout trainers cite; AoPS host 403-blocks bots but URL is public & canonical.

items.label: Evan Chen — A Brief Introduction to Olympiad Inequalities
items.why: Evan Chen (IMO gold, OTIS founder) — canon handout; evanchen.cc 403-blocks bots but is public & free.

items.label: Evan Chen — Olympiad Articles (handout index: inequalities, SOS, Monsters, polynomials)
items.why: Central index of Chen's free olympiad handouts (Olympiad Inequalities, SOS, Summation, Monsters); 403 to bots, public.

items.label: imomath (Djukić/Matić) — free training texts: Polynomials, Equations in Polynomials, Inequalities
items.why: Written by IMO Compendium authors; verified live, free topic articles on polynomials & inequalities.
items.extra: free · IMO-level · authors of the Compendium

items.label: Michael Penn — YouTube (olympiad algebra/number-theory problem walkthroughs)
items.why: Math professor; deep, rigorous olympiad-problem videos (algebra, NT, FE) — channel confirmed via search.

items.label: Math Olympiad Inequalities — Essential Techniques (playlist)
items.why: Topic-focused playlist on the core inequality methods (AM-GM, Cauchy, SOS) for olympiad training.

items.label: IMO Shortlist Problems by year (AoPS Wiki) — Algebra (A-codes)
items.why: Master index to every shortlist year; A1–A8 algebra problems with community solutions; AoPS 403s bots, public.

items.label: imomath — IMO Problem Collection (by year, PDF)
items.why: Verified-live free year-by-year IMO problem PDFs (314+ problems) from the Compendium authors.

items.label: IMO official — all IMO problems by year (multilingual PDFs)
items.why: The authoritative source; verified-live year dropdown 1959–2025 plus official shortlists (released post-IMO).

items.label: AoPS — High School Olympiads forum
items.why: The global olympiad community; every shortlist/national algebra problem has a discussion thread; 403s bots, public.

items.label: Justin Stevens — Olympiad Number Theory Through Challenging Problems (author site, 4th ed.)
items.why: Author-hosted FREE book (verified live), all editions; divisibility→p-adic→Diophantine, with companion videos.

items.label: Justin Stevens — Olympiad NT (3rd ed. PDF, AoPS CDN)
items.why: Direct PDF verified valid (%PDF); the widely-shared 120-page edition, stable AoPS CDN link.

items.label: Yufei Zhao — Modular Arithmetic handout
items.why: Direct PDF verified valid; Fermat/Euler/Wilson and residue classes from the canonical handout set.

items.label: Yufei Zhao — aⁿ ± 1 / Lifting the Exponent (LTE)
items.why: Direct PDF verified valid; the definitive free LTE-lemma handout, a staple NT olympiad technique.

items.label: imomath (Djukić) — Pell's equation, Quadratic congruences, Arithmetic in extensions of ℚ
items.why: Compendium-author NT texts (Pell, quadratic residues, algebraic NT); verified-live training page.
items.extra: free · IMO-level · authors of the Compendium

items.label: The IMO Compendium 1959–2009 (full book, Internet Archive)
items.why: Free legal scan of Djukić-Janković-Matić-Petrović — every IMO + shortlist problem solved (the old imocompendium.com is now parked).

items.label: Michael Penn — YouTube (number-theory olympiad problems)
items.why: Rigorous, frequent NT olympiad walkthroughs (national + IMO problems); channel URL confirmed via search.

items.label: Aditya Khurmi — Chinese Remainder Theorem (olympiad NT lecture)
items.why: Author of Modern Olympiad NT giving a clean CRT lecture with handout; respected olympiad instructor.

items.label: IMO Shortlist Problems by year (AoPS Wiki) — Number Theory (N-codes)
items.why: N1–N8 number-theory shortlist problems by year with linked community solutions; AoPS 403s bots, public.

items.label: IMO official — problems & shortlists by year
items.why: Authoritative official PDFs incl. shortlists; verified-live; best primary source for real exam NT problems.

items.label: AoPS Wiki — IMO Problems and Solutions (hub)
items.why: Per-problem solution pages for every IMO incl. NT; URL confirmed via search; AoPS 403s bots, public.

items.label: AoPS — High School Olympiads forum (Number Theory threads)
items.why: Where every shortlist N-problem gets multiple solutions debated; the de-facto olympiad discussion hub; 403s bots, public.

clusters.cluster: IMO Combinatorics, Geometry & Functional Equations — premium free resources
clusters.summary: Verified-live premium free resources for three IMO subjects, each in four groups (Learn / Watch / Practice / Discuss). The canon dominates: Evan Chen's free handouts + EGMO free subset (geometry) and FuncEq/Monsters (FE), Yufei Zhao's MIT olympiad handouts (combinatorics + geometry), the IMOmath training texts by the IMO Compendium authors (FE, combinatorics, projective geometry), plus official IMO/Shortlist problem archives, AoPS (community + wiki + Alcumus), and a few top YouTube channels. Every non-AoPS URL was fetched and returned HTTP 200. AoPS pages return 403 to automated fetchers (Cloudflare bot-block) but are public and the gold-standard olympiad forum — flagged in each 'why'. Anything unverifiable was left as "".

items.label: Yufei Zhao — Combinatorics handouts (comb1, comb3, algebraic techniques, tiling, bijections, counting in two ways)
items.why: MIT prof, longtime USA IMO team coach; these MOP training handouts are a universally recommended olympiad combinatorics canon

items.label: Evan Chen — The Probabilistic Method (handout)
items.why: Evan Chen (USA IMO coach, OTIS founder) — the standard concise intro to a core olympiad combinatorics technique

items.label: IMOmath — Combinatorics (Matić & Novaković)
items.why: Written by the IMO Compendium team; rigorous free training text covering counting, pigeonhole, extremal and invariants

items.label: Michael Penn — combinatorics & olympiad problem videos
items.why: Math professor; clean, rigorous worked solutions to competition combinatorics problems, large well-organized library

items.label: AoPS Wiki — IMO Shortlist Problems (combinatorics C-problems archive)
items.why: The definitive curated problem set olympiad trainers drill; each problem links to community solution threads

items.label: IMOmath — Olympiad problems database (filter by Combinatorics)
items.why: Large searchable archive of national/IMO olympiad problems by topic from the IMO Compendium authors

items.label: AoPS — High School Olympiads forum
items.why: The single most active olympiad discussion community worldwide; combinatorics problems get expert solutions fast

items.label: Evan Chen — EGMO (Euclidean Geometry in Math Olympiads): free subset — TOC, Chapters 1–3 preview, full errata/solutions (AGEST), Trig-Bash course
items.why: THE canonical olympiad-geometry textbook; the full book is paid but the author posts a substantial free subset + auto-generated solutions treasury
items.extra: free subset · book is paid · IMO level

items.label: Evan Chen — AGEST: Automatically Generated EGMO Solutions Treasury
items.why: Free worked solutions to a large fraction of EGMO's problems — turns the paid book's exercises into self-study material

items.label: Yufei Zhao — Geometry handouts (Lemmas in Euclidean Geometry, Cyclic Quadrilaterals, Three Lemmas, Power of a Point, Similarity/Spiral Similarity)
items.why: MIT/USA-IMO-coach handouts; the 'Lemmas' and 'Cyclic Quadrilaterals' notes are a staple first stop for olympiad geometry

items.label: Evan Chen — Directed Angles (handout)
items.why: The standard short treatment of directed (mod 180°) angles — a technique essential for rigorous configuration-independent geo proofs

items.label: IMOmath — Projective Geometry (Lukić) & Inversion (Djukić)
items.why: IMO Compendium authors' free advanced-geometry texts; projective + inversion are exactly the hard-olympiad tools EGMO covers late

items.label: Evan Chen — AsyGuide (Asymptote diagram tutorial for olympiad geometry)
items.why: Author of EGMO walks through drawing accurate olympiad-geometry figures; the standard reference for the field's diagram tool

items.label: Michael Penn — olympiad geometry problem videos
items.why: Rigorous, well-paced video solutions to competition geometry; large indexed library

items.label: Evan Chen — tr011ey: 'Undergraduate Math 011, a first-year course in geometry' (free problem-rich notes)
items.why: Free standalone geometry course by the EGMO author — builds the synthetic toolkit through exercises

items.label: AoPS Wiki — IMO Shortlist Problems (geometry G-problems archive)
items.why: The curated geometry problem ladder olympiad trainers assign, with community solutions per problem

items.label: AoPS — High School Olympiads forum
items.why: Largest olympiad-geometry discussion hub; synthetic/projective/bary solutions debated by strong solvers

items.label: Evan Chen — Introduction to Functional Equations (FuncEq-Intro handout)
items.why: USA-IMO-coach's go-to FE primer: substitution, injectivity/surjectivity, Cauchy — the standard first FE handout

items.label: Evan Chen — Monsters: pathological functional equations (handout)
items.why: The reference handout on the wild non-continuous (Cauchy-pathology) FE solutions that trap competitors — advanced and widely cited

items.label: IMOmath — Functional Equations (Marko Radovanović)
items.why: IMO Compendium author's free text: 'twelve ways to treat FEs', Cauchy equation, plus classified solved problems and practice set

items.label: Michael Penn — functional equations videos
items.why: Clear worked video solutions of competition FEs showing the substitution-and-deduce method end to end

items.label: AoPS Wiki — IMO Shortlist Problems (algebra A-problems, where FEs live)
items.why: FE problems are the core of Shortlist Algebra; this curated archive is the standard FE training set with solution threads

items.label: IMOmath — Olympiad problems database (FE / algebra filter)
items.why: Searchable national+IMO problem archive to drill FEs by source and difficulty

items.label: AoPS — High School Olympiads forum
items.why: FE problems get rapid expert critique here — ideal for checking whether your FE proof actually pins down all solutions

items.label: IMO Official — problems & results archive (all years, all problems)
items.why: The authoritative primary source for every IMO problem and country results — the canonical practice corpus

items.label: AoPS — Alcumus (adaptive practice, incl. counting/probability & geometry)
items.why: Free adaptive problem trainer from AoPS; strong for building pre-olympiad combinatorics/geometry fundamentals

items.label: IMOmath — The IMO Compendium (book page) & full training-texts index
items.why: Hub of free olympiad training texts (FE, combinatorics, projective geo, inversion, polynomials) by the IMO Compendium authors

items.label: Evan Chen — full olympiad handout index (geo/combo/FE/NT/algebra)
items.why: One page linking every free Evan Chen handout across all olympiad subjects — the canonical free olympiad library

items.label: vEnhance (Evan Chen)
items.why: IMO gold and OTIS founder; lectures and live solves

items.label: Michael Penn
items.why: Competition and number-theory problems solved cleanly

items.label: 3Blue1Brown
items.why: Visual math intuition for olympiad insight

items.label: Mathologer
items.why: Rigorous theorem and proof dives

items.label: Po-Shen Loh Daily Challenge
items.why: USA IMO head coach; live competition solves

items.label: Online Math Club
items.why: Student-run olympiad seminars with handouts

items.label: AoPS Community
items.why: Gold-standard olympiad forum; every problem discussed

items.label: AoPS Wiki
items.why: Theorems, techniques and AMC-IMO archives

items.label: Math Olympiads Discord MODS
items.why: Largest reputable olympiad Discord; daily problems

items.label: IMO Official
items.why: Official IMO problems and Shortlist PDFs

items.label: Evan Chen web.evanchen.cc
items.why: Free library: handouts, OTIS Excerpts, EGMO, Napkin

items.label: Yufei Zhao Olympiad handouts
items.why: MIT and ex-IMO coach free training handouts

items.label: Terence Tao What's New
items.why: Fields Medalist and ex-IMO gold; expository writing

items.label: IMO Compendium IMOmath
items.why: IMO Compendium 1959-2009 plus free training texts

items.label: Cut-the-Knot
items.why: Classic archive of proofs and olympiad geometry

clusters.summary: A "Winners" gallery of 22 notable IMO competitors spanning the 1960s/1980s through the 2020s, chosen to inspire students and give teachers concrete "where did they go" stories. The careers angle is front-and-center: Fields Medalists (Tao, Perelman, Mirzakhani, Ngo, Scholze, Avila, Drinfeld), Abel/Wolf/Shaw/Knuth laureates, AI/tech (Noam Shazeer — Transformer co-inventor, Character.AI, Gemini; Nipun Pitimanaaree — DeFi CTO), academia (Manolescu at Stanford, Sauermann at Bonn, Reiher at Hamburg, Babai/Lovász in CS), an economist (Gabriel Carroll), the legendary US coach & edtech founder (Po-Shen Loh), the olympiad-teacher/author archetype (Evan Chen, OTIS), and even a head of state (Nicușor Dan, President of Romania). Records are verified against Wikipedia / official IMO pages. Highlights: Manolescu is the only 3x perfect-scorer; Sauermann and Reid Barton are among the handful of 4-gold winners; Tao won gold at 13 (youngest ever); Zhuo Qun Song holds the record 5 golds. Note: all-time-great status and "now" careers were confirmed as of mid-2026 (e.g., Shazeer's 2026 move).

items.label: Terence Tao
items.why: The most famous living IMO alum and a generational mathematician; the single best inspirational name for students.
items.extra: 🇦🇺 Australia · Gold 1988 at age 13 (youngest-ever gold), also bronze 1986 & silver 1987 · now: Fields Medalist (2006), professor at UCLA, one of the world's most prolific mathematicians

items.label: Grigori Perelman
items.why: Perfect IMO score who went on to settle one of the seven Millennium Prize Problems — a legend students know by name.
items.extra: 🇷🇺 Soviet Union · Perfect score 42/42, Gold 1982 · now: proved the Poincaré Conjecture; declined both the Fields Medal (2006) and the $1M Millennium Prize

items.label: Maryam Mirzakhani
items.why: First and (so far) only woman to win the Fields Medal — the defining role model for girls in olympiad math.
items.extra: 🇮🇷 Iran · Gold 1994, Gold + perfect score 1995 · now (legacy): first female Fields Medalist (2014), Stanford professor (d. 2017)

items.label: Ngô Bảo Châu
items.why: Two-time gold and the first Vietnamese Fields Medalist — a national-hero example of olympiad-to-research.
items.extra: 🇻🇳 Vietnam · Gold + perfect score 1988, Gold 1989 · now: Fields Medalist (2010) for the Fundamental Lemma; professor at the University of Chicago

items.label: Peter Scholze
items.why: Four-time gold who became one of the youngest Fields Medalists ever — a modern olympiad-to-superstar arc.
items.extra: 🇩🇪 Germany · 3 Gold + 1 Silver (2004–2007), perfect score 2005 · now: Fields Medalist (2018), director at the Max Planck Institute, Bonn

items.label: Artur Avila
items.why: First Latin-American Fields Medalist; shows the olympiad pipeline reaching beyond the usual countries.
items.extra: 🇧🇷 Brazil · Gold 1995 · now: Fields Medalist (2014) in dynamical systems; professor at the University of Zürich and IMPA

items.label: Vladimir Drinfeld
items.why: An all-time great from the 1960s era — perfect IMO score who later swept the field's top prizes over decades.
items.extra: 🇷🇺 Soviet Union · Gold + perfect score 1969 (age 15) · now: Fields Medal (1990), Wolf Prize (2018), Shaw Prize (2023); professor at the University of Chicago

items.label: László Lovász
items.why: A 1960s multi-gold legend who became a pillar of combinatorics and theoretical CS — bridges math and computer science.
items.extra: 🇭🇺 Hungary · 3 Gold (incl. perfect scores) 1964–1966 · now: Abel Prize (2021), Wolf Prize, Knuth Prize; former president of the IMU

items.label: Ciprian Manolescu
items.why: The only person ever to score a perfect 42 three times — the headline 'most decorated scorer' story.
items.extra: 🇷🇴 Romania · Perfect score 42/42 all three years (1995, 1996, 1997) · now: professor at Stanford; resolved the Triangulation Conjecture (2019 Moore Prize)

items.label: Lisa Sauermann
items.why: Among the most successful contestants ever and the leading female multi-gold winner — a top inspirational figure for girls.
items.extra: 🇩🇪 Germany · 4 Gold + 1 Silver (2007–2011), perfect score & sole top scorer 2011 · now: holds a Hausdorff Chair at the University of Bonn (combinatorics)

items.label: Reid Barton
items.why: First-ever four-time gold medalist and the only person to win gold at both the IMO and the IOI — a dual math/CS prodigy.
items.extra: 🇺🇸 USA · 4 consecutive Gold (1998–2001), perfect score 2001; also IOI golds · now: mathematician, 4-time Putnam Fellow, Harvard PhD

items.label: Gabriel Carroll
items.why: Perfect-scorer who switched fields — proof that olympiad skill transfers far beyond pure math.
items.extra: 🇺🇸 USA · Gold + perfect score 2001 · now: economics professor (University of Toronto), microeconomic theory & mechanism design

items.label: Noam Shazeer
items.why: The headline AI/tech story: an IMO gold who co-invented the Transformer that powers modern AI — directly relevant and motivating today.
items.extra: 🇺🇸 USA · Gold + perfect score 1994 · now: co-author of 'Attention Is All You Need' (the Transformer), founder of Character.AI, Google Gemini co-lead

items.label: László Babai
items.why: Perfect-scorer turned foundational theoretical-computer-scientist — another strong math-to-CS career example.
items.extra: 🇭🇺 Hungary · Gold + perfect score 1968 · now: professor at the University of Chicago; Knuth Prize (2015) for work on complexity & the graph isomorphism problem

items.label: Christian Reiher
items.why: One of the rare four-gold winners who stayed in research mathematics — a clean academia trajectory.
items.extra: 🇩🇪 Germany · 4 Gold + 1 Bronze (1999–2003) · now: professor at the University of Hamburg, extremal combinatorics & graph theory

items.label: Nicușor Dan
items.why: Two perfect scores who became a head of state — the most surprising 'where did they go' story for students.
items.extra: 🇷🇴 Romania · 2 Gold + perfect scores (1987, 1988) · now: President of Romania (2025); mathematics PhD, former Mayor of Bucharest

items.label: Po-Shen Loh
items.why: The most relevant figure for teachers: silver medalist turned legendary US team coach and math-education entrepreneur.
items.extra: 🇺🇸 USA · Silver 1999 · now: Carnegie Mellon professor, US IMO head coach 2014–2023 (team won 2015/16/18/19), founder of edtech expii.com

items.label: Evan Chen
items.why: The modern olympiad-teacher archetype: gold medalist, textbook author, and founder of a hugely popular training program — directly useful to coaching teachers.
items.extra: 🇺🇸 USA (Taiwanese-American) · Gold 2014 · now: MIT number-theory PhD, author of 'Euclidean Geometry in Math Olympiads', founder of the OTIS olympiad-training program

items.label: Zhuo Qun (Alex) Song
items.why: Holds the all-time record of five gold medals — the 'most golds ever' headline for a leaderboard.
items.extra: 🇨🇦 Canada · 5 Gold + 1 Bronze (2010–2015), record total · now: graduate researcher in number theory (UIUC)

items.label: Luke Robitaille
items.why: A current-era four-time gold medalist — a fresh 2020s name students may recognize from recent teams.
items.extra: 🇺🇸 USA · 4 Gold (2019–2022), gold in every appearance · now: studies mathematics & physics at MIT

items.label: Nipun Pitimanaaree
items.why: Four-gold winner who went into deep tech / fintech — shows the olympiad path leading into startups and crypto.
items.extra: 🇹🇭 Thailand · 4 Gold + 1 Silver (2009–2013) · now: co-founder and CTO of INFINIT, a decentralized-finance venture

items.label: Teodor von Burg
items.why: A four-gold winner who returned to teaching the next generation — a relatable role-model-as-teacher example.
items.extra: 🇷🇸 Serbia · 4 Gold + 1 Silver + 1 Bronze (2007–2012) · now: teaches specialized mathematics in Belgrade and tutors olympiad students
